generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor driving the first vertical axis of MABI MAX 100 robot"
]

instance GearboxActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Gear transmission component in the first axis assembly of MABI MAX 100 robot"
]

instance CycloidalGearboxActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox component in the first axis assembly of MABI MAX 100 robot"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Encoder mounted directly on the motor to measure motor position and velocity"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionData
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorVelocityData
]

instance GearboxEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary encoder positioned behind the gearbox to measure gearbox output angle and reduce positional uncertainty"
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxAngleData
]

instance InputCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring input current (I_M) to the motor via MQTT protocol"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InputCurrentData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance MotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance MotorVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance GearboxAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxEncoder
]

instance InputCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InputCurrentSensor
]

instance TorqueEstimationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived data from motor current measurements using manufacturer models"
    DTDFVocab:fromData InputCurrentData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTProtocol
]

instance AngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance StructuralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) and Internal Block Diagram (IBD) describing the first axis as composition of Motor → Gearbox → Cycloidal Gearbox"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLTool
]

instance IdealDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulation model in MATLAB Simulink showing ideal dynamics (without friction) for the first axis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model implementing Coulomb-Viscous friction components for the first axis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model implementing Stribeck friction model for the first axis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model implementing LuGre friction model for the first axis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance MeasuredTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data from sensor streams capturing actual torque measurements from motor and gearbox"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TorqueEstimationData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTProtocol
]

instance SimulatedTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Output from Simulink friction models representing predicted torque values"
    DTDFVocab:fromData CoulombViscousFrictionModel
    DTDFVocab:fromData StribeckFrictionModel
    DTDFVocab:fromData LuGreFrictionModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance MeasuredVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data from sensor streams capturing actual angular velocity measurements"
    DTDFVocab:fromData AngularVelocityData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTProtocol
]

instance SimulatedVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Output from Simulink models representing predicted angular velocity values"
    DTDFVocab:fromData IdealDynamicsModel
    DTDFVocab:fromData CoulombViscousFrictionModel
    DTDFVocab:fromData StribeckFrictionModel
    DTDFVocab:fromData LuGreFrictionModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance MetadataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Metadata capturing source tracing for sensor channels and computational methods (e.g., sensor channel, computational method)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MeasuredTorqueData
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulatedTorqueData
    DTDFVocab:fromData MeasuredVelocityData
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulatedVelocityData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MetadataTool
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML modeling tool used for structural/behavioral modeling of the robot's first axis"
    DTDFVocab:enables StructuralModel
]

instance SimulinkTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB Simulink used for executable friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, LuGre)"
    DTDFVocab:enables IdealDynamicsModel
    DTDFVocab:enables CoulombViscousFrictionModel
    DTDFVocab:enables StribeckFrictionModel
    DTDFVocab:enables LuGreFrictionModel
]

instance MQTTProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol for real-time sensor data streaming (input current I_M, output angle)"
    DTDFVocab:enables MeasuredTorqueData
    DTDFVocab:enables MeasuredVelocityData
]

instance MetadataTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Tool for capturing and managing metadata traces for data traceability"
    DTDFVocab:enables MetadataTrace
]

instance CustomDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom visualization dashboard for integrating and visualizing data traces (measured vs. simulated torque/velocity)"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time comparison of simulated friction models with live measurements to dynamically adjust friction parameters"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionModelRefinementInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionAnomalyDetectionAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integrated visualization of data traces (measured vs. simulated torque/velocity) for operator monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:provides DashboardVisualizationInsight
]

instance ControlOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of friction models to reduce motion inaccuracies (up to 30%)"
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionAccuracyImprovementAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionModelRefinementInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of friction model parameters through real-time comparison of simulated vs. measured data"
]

instance FrictionAnomalyDetectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Alerts operators to anomalies (wear, tooling changes) via dashboard visualizations"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DashboardVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of measured vs. simulated torque/velocity data for anomaly identification"
]

instance MotionAccuracyImprovementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adjusts friction models dynamically to reduce motion inaccuracies (up to 30%)"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance OperationalMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring of system performance to detect wear and tooling changes"
]

instance ControlParameterAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of control parameters based on friction model refinement"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale is **real-time and dynamic**, with:
  - **Data acquisition frequency**: Sensor data (e.g., motor current, encoders) is streamed in **real-time** via MQTT, enabling **continuous, incremental updates** to the digital shadow.
  - **Model update frequency**: Friction models (white-box/black-box) are dynamically refined during operation, with adjustments triggered by deviations between measured and simulated data traces.
  - **Synchronization**: The digital shadow processes data in **sub-second intervals** to maintain alignment with physical system dynamics (e.g., robot motion cycles)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital shadow is a **standalone, purpose-driven component** within a broader **digital twin constellation** for the MABI MAX 100 robot. It serves as a foundational layer for:
  - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: Enables integration of the digital shadow with the broader DT ecosystem (Section I).
  - **Multi-physics integration**: Links structural (mechanical), behavioral (friction), and simulation models to support comprehensive robot modeling.
  - **Orchestration**: The digital shadow’s data and models are used to inform higher-level DT services (e.g., control optimization, task planning) but operates independently for friction analysis."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital shadow operates across the following **life-cycle stages**:
  - **As-designed (ideal)**: Structural and behavioral models (e.g., SysML component diagrams) describe the robot’s first axis architecture and ideal dynamics (Fig. 3).
  - **As-manufactured**: Real-time sensor data and live measurements (e.g., motor current, encoders) are captured during operation to validate and refine friction models.
  - **As-operated**: Continuous monitoring and model updates ensure the digital shadow adapts to operational changes (e.g., wear, tooling variations) to maintain motion accuracy."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Verification**:
    - **Sensor calibration**: Encoder readings (motor/gearbox) reduce positional uncertainty by comparing encoder outputs.
    - **Model validation**: Simulink models (e.g., Fig. 4) are validated against live measurements to validate friction parameter accuracy.
  - **Uncertainty Handling**:
    - **Data traceability**: Metadata links each data point to its source (e.g., sensor channel, computational method), enabling error attribution.
    - **Dynamic adaptation**: Friction models are updated in real-time to account for wear, tooling changes, or operational variations.
  - **Validation Mechanisms**:
    - **Deviation analysis**: Differences between measured and simulated data (e.g., torque/velocity) highlight friction anomalies.
    - **Dashboard visualization**: Operators can validate model accuracy via integrated comparisons."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The digital shadow’s technical connection includes:
  - **Network Protocol**: **MQTT** for real-time sensor data streaming from the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D CNC controller.
  - **Data Flow**: One-way data flow (passive digital shadow) from physical sensors to processing components (e.g., Simulink models) for friction analysis.
  - **Integration Architecture**: The digital shadow is designed to interact with the broader **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** for scalability to other robot axes."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital shadow is hosted and deployed as:
  - **Real-time processing**: Data is processed in **sub-second intervals** to maintain synchronization with physical robot motion.
  - **Dashboard visualization**: Interactive visualization tools (e.g., custom dashboards) display data traces for operator inspection.
  - **Model execution**: Simulink models run on **local/embedded systems** (e.g., robot controller or dedicated DT server) to avoid latency."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The digital shadow integrates horizontally with:
  - **External systems**: The **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** enables integration with other DTs or enterprise systems (e.g., PLCs, ERP) for broader manufacturing data exchange.
  - **Data aggregation**: Links to other sensor streams (e.g., other robot axes) for multi-axis friction modeling in future extensions."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital shadow adheres to:
  - **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)**: Follows the **UML-based reference model** (Fig. 1) for structure (e.g., Purpose, DataTrace, Model types: Engineering/Simulation/DataCalculation).
  - **SysML standards**: Uses **SysML Block Definition Diagrams (BDDs)** and **Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)** for structural modeling (Section II-B).
  - **MathWorks Simulink**: Leverages industry-standard simulation tools for friction modeling (Fig. 4)."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The digital shadow includes:
  - **Data security**: MQTT protocol ensures secure transmission of sensor data (though specifics on encryption are not detailed).
  - **Safety considerations**: The digital shadow operates in a **passive mode** (one-way data flow), avoiding autonomous control risks. However, no explicit mention of remote execution or human-robot interaction safety protocols is provided."
]
